CONTENT.Club career: Sala came to German side Hamburger SV from Chelsea on a three-year contract, joining former Chelsea teammate Michael Mancienne at the club. He made his competitive debut for Hamburg in a 5–1 home defeat against Borussia Dortmund; he came on as a substitute in the 65th minute, replacing Marcell Jansen.On 24 July 2013, he joined Hellas Verona. Sala made his debut on 1 September 2013, against Roma; the match ended in a 3–0 defeat for Verona.On 30 January 2016, Sala joined Sampdoria on loan with an obligation to buy.On 2 September 2019, Sala signed to SPAL.On 5 September 2020, Sala signed to Spezia a 2-years contract. He was the first deal for Spezia in Serie A. On 1 September 2023, his contract with Spezia was terminated by mutual consent.On 22 January 2024, Sala joined Rimini in Serie C on a contract until June 2025.
CONTENT.International career: On 15 August 2012, Sala made his debut with the Italy U-21 team in a friendly match against the Netherlands.

SUMMARY: Jacopo Sala (Italian pronunciation: [ˈjaːkopo ˈsaːla]; born 5 December 1991) is an Italian footballer who plays as a central midfielder for Serie C Group B club Rimini.
